collected.policy_snippet: For example, if you choose not to save any voice recordings, voice IDs associated with your account will be deleted and voice ID will not work. Similarly, if you delete the voice recordings Alexa used to create your voice ID (by, for example, choosing to have your Alexa voice recordings deleted automatically after 3 or 18 months), voice ID may not work
collected.purpose: The term Voice ID strongly indicate there is identification of voice recordings, making this biometric data rather than rather than a simple voice recording not used for identification purposes. For further insight into what Voice ID is, the policy refers to a not present FAQ.

data_minimization.reasoning: The policy does not describe concrete limitations on how this biometric data is collected and to which extent this data is handled. It describes usage of voice recordings for machine learning purposes and states that they may use interactions to improve other amazon services. There are no clear statements on how minimization occurs on this data.

stored.policy_snippet: No audio is stored or sent to the cloud unless the device detects the wake word (or Alexa is activated by pressing a button)
stored.purpose: Indicate that voice recording ar enot sent or stored to the cloud unless Alexa is activated, inferring ID related voice recording are stored to the cloud if the Alexa product is used or activated.

retention_policy.policy_snippet_deletion: Yes. You can review and delete Alexa voice recordings, text transcripts of those recordings
retention_policy.policy_snippet_inactivity: Yes, you can choose to automatically delete your voice recordings, text transcripts, and typed requests to Alexa after a specified period of time (3 or 18 months), or you can choose to not save any voice recordings.

shared.policy_snippet: As we continue to develop our business, we might sell or buy other businesses or services.
shared.purpose: It is not clearly stated when sharing of voice recording occurs.

collected.policy_snippet: By default, Alexa-enabled devices are designed to detect only your chosen wake word (e.g., Alexa, Amazon, Computer, Echo, or Ziggy). The device detects the wake word by identifying acoustic patterns that match the wake word. No audio is stored or sent to the cloud unless the device detects the wake word (or Alexa is activated by pressing a button).
collected.purpose: No guarantee that audio is stored/transferred all the time.

stored.policy_snippet: When you use our voice, image and camera services, we use your voice input, images, videos, and other personal information to respond to your requests, provide the requested service to you, and improve our services.

retention_policy.policy_snippet_deletion: Yes, you can choose to automatically delete your voice recordings, text transcripts, and typed requests to Alexa after a specified period of time (3 or 18 months), or you can choose to not save any voice recordings.

physiological_data.overall_privacy_risk_description: In general, a device that is constantly listening to our voice and is occationally recording/processing/transfering it, must be seen as a privacy risk. The user has not control for the device is actually doing.

collected.policy_snippet: Our physical stores may use cameras, computer vision, sensors, and other technology to gather information about your activity in the store, such as the products and services you interact with.
collected.purpose: Amazon states that they may collect information from cameraes, sensors and other technology, on top of voice recordings. This will include images, and likely a wide range of other data about a person considering amazons wide range of services such asamazo

stored.policy_snippet: images and videos collected or stored in connection with Amazon Services

retention_policy.policy_snippet_deletion: Yes. You can review and delete Alexa voice recordings, text transcripts of those recordings
retention_policy.policy_snippet_inactivity: As we continue to develop our business, we might sell or buy other businesses or services.

collected.policy_snippet: Our physical stores may use cameras, computer vision, sensors, and other technology to gather information about your activity in the store, such as the products and services you interact with.
